title: Support DOCKER_HOST and DOCKER_CERT_PATH
body:
The assumed format for DOCKER_HOST is `<schema>://<host>[:<port>][path]` .

If the ssh schema is used for DOCKER_HOST, "/run/podman/podman.sock"
is appended if path is not given.  All other schemas are assumed to
resolve to a destination running a Podman service as given.

usage: `$ DOCKER_HOST=ssh://root@localhost:22 bin/podman ps --all`

Issue: GH-7010
Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This is confusing and incompatible. Please consider choosing other variable name.
@AkihiroSuda Not sure what variable names you are saying CONTAINER_HOST and CONTAINER_SSHKEY?  These are already released, Although really not documented.

> This is confusing and incompatible. Please consider choosing other variable name.

@AkihiroSuda, can you elaborate on that?

> $ DOCKER_HOST=ssh://root@localhost:22 bin/podman ps --all

I'd expect this command to connect to the Docker REST API via `ssh <USER>@<HOST> -- docker system dial-stdio`, which typically proxies `/var/run/docker.sock`.
https://github.com/docker/cli/blob/f4f962292d4762c36460ac2cc5c294af6903061f/cli/connhelper/connhelper.go#L20-L45

@AkihiroSuda Our customers have asked us to support DOCKER_HOST in the podman code to simplify supporting both products in their environment.

Your example provides a value for DOCKER_HOST consistent with this PR. The actual implementation of passing the bits between client and service is different between podman and docker.  We leverage systemd's socket activation protocol on the service side vs writing/embedding a proxy.

> The actual implementation of passing the bits between client and service is different between podman and docker.

I think ssh:// should be only supported in $CONTAINER_HOST then.

When ssh:// is specified in $DOCKER_HOST, Podman should error out or at least it should print logrus.Warn("Connecting to Podman, not Docker")

> @afbjorklund PTAL

I don't have any plans of using DOCKER_HOST for connecting to _podman_, but welcome not having to specify socket path...

Suppose there could be a problem with path if you want to support the regular system socket and rootless user sockets
But as we are mostly using it as cri-o interoperability, defaulting the path to `/run/podman/podman.sock` would be fine.

> - `user` will default to either `root` or current running user

> - `path` defaults to either `/run/podman/podman.sock`, or `/run/user/<uid>/podman/podman.sock` if running rootless.

When using something like minikube or vagrant, it uses a regular user for ssh access and then sudo/group for access.
This means that this proposed scheme would fail to detect the path, since it would try to run rootless if user is not "root".

It seems like DOCKER_CERT_PATH only works for certificates and not for ssh keys, so used `ssh-agent` for those instead.
Not sure where to configure host key handling, so I guess that has to be handled outside of this client ssh configuration ?

----

I documented my "machine" replacement here: https://boot2podman.github.io/2020/07/22/machine-replacement.html

`export CONTAINER_HOST=ssh://vagrant@127.0.0.1:2222/run/podman/podman.sock`

`export DOCKER_HOST=ssh://vagrant@127.0.0.1:2222 # /var/run/docker.sock`

Trying to specify the path for docker (in DOCKER_HOST) currently causes a runtime error, so it is just a comment above.

This was as a replacement for the deprecated podman-machine/docker-machine, with the old varlink and port 2376 access...
Previously it (docker) would use something like `tcp://192.168.99.100:2376`, but this is now deprecated in favor of `ssh://`

There are multiple network interfaces, so it prefers tunneling ssh over 2222 rather than just using `ssh://192.168.99.100:22`
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
I am taking this PR over,  #7620 to get it merged.
Well actually I am rethinking the DOCKER_HOST support altogether and just grabbing the connection documentation.
